meta.note: Rest-of-LatAm sweep (Andes + Central America). Thin records. New country row added for Honduras. Ecuador's 'El Gran Hermano' excluded (a damages judgment, not a ban); El Salvador, Bolivia, Costa Rica, Panama yielded nothing title-specific. Peru's two entries are a 2025 Indecopi consumer-protection ruling restricting titles in one private school's library (scope=school), not a national ban.

entries.title: Venezuela bajo el signo del terror 1948-1952
entries.description_ban: Clandestine exposé ('El libro negro de la dictadura') of torture and concentration camps under Pérez Jiménez; printing workshops were repeatedly shut by the Seguridad Nacional and the editor Catalá was jailed in 1953 for printing it.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific persecution by the dictatorship; documented via Spanish Wikipedia with citations.
entries.source_name: Wikipedia (es) — El libro negro de la dictadura

entries.title: La metamorfosis de su excelencia
entries.description_ban: Satire of President Mariano Ospina Pérez; censored by his government, and Zalamea was jailed for two weeks in solitary confinement.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific state ban; documented by El Tiempo.

entries.title: Geografía general de los Estados Unidos de Colombia
entries.description_ban: Suppressed by President Tomás Cipriano de Mosquera, who held that it favoured Brazil's Amazon territorial claims.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific 19th-century state ban; documented by Semana (medium confidence).

entries.title: La Amazonia colombiana
entries.description_ban: Ordered withdrawn over Amazon-border territorial concerns under Marco Fidel Suárez; only a few copies survive. Exact year approximate.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific withdrawal over territorial sensitivities; documented by Semana (medium confidence).

entries.title: Gender Queer: A Memoir
entries.description_ban: Among 21 titles Peru's consumer-protection agency Indecopi (Resolution 2908-2025/CC2) ordered restricted in the secondary library of Colegio Roosevelt, fining the school for 'inappropriate content'. A government-agency action naming specific titles, but scope is one private school, not a national ban.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Government-agency restriction naming the title; reported by El Comercio (scope: single school).

entries.title: Flamer
entries.description_ban: Cited specifically in the same 2025 Indecopi/Colegio Roosevelt ruling as a 'sexually explicit graphic novel'; restricted in one private school's library, not nationally banned.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Government-agency restriction naming the title; reported by El Comercio (scope: single school).

entries.title: Prisión Verde
entries.description_ban: Social-realist novel exposing labour conditions on the Standard Fruit banana plantations; long prohibited in Honduras and described as 'the most persecuted book in the country', with possession used as evidence for imprisonment. Ban spans the Carías dictatorship and later military rule.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific long-running ban; documented via Wikipedia and the author's estate.
